top_concerns: Core novelty over prior MT constraint, relaxation, and applicability work needs sharper differentiation
Empirical section is sprawling and mixes primary evidence with many secondary stress/audit claims
Several coverage and fault-detection claims remain based on authored mutants, synthetic/OOD cases, and non-independent cells
Presentation is dense and may obscure the central contribution for IST readers
Overly narrow empirical scope limits generalizability claims
Seeded-fault results presented as stress tests but risk over-interpretation as coverage evidence
Some tables (e.g., claim-to-evidence) belong in appendix rather than main text
Minor over-emphasis on methodological novelty vs. incremental advance on prior MT constraint work
Limited generalization beyond studied CFD and SciML surrogate families
Deferred or incomplete verdicts for some conservation relations due to numerical floor calibration
Typology and numerical decidability axes require further calibration and validation
Reproducibility dependent on some proprietary or heavyweight dependencies
Limited external validity beyond CFD and specific surrogate families
Verdict interpretation relies on per-relation domain metrics not cross-calibrated
Coverage implications remain qualitative and bounded to seeded faults
